data.title: Zedge outlines disciplined innovation and monetization strategy while expanding AI data offerings
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Zedge, Inc. (ZDGE) Q2 2026

Management View

  • CEO Jonathan Reich emphasized that "the quality of our monetization continues to improve, and this is leading to record results." He noted Zedge achieved record levels of revenue and average revenue per monthly active user, attributing gains to "continued advertising optimization, record active subscription numbers and record Zedge Premium GTV." Reich also highlighted a focus on acquiring higher-value users and driving resilience in the marketplace despite continued contraction in monthly active users.
  • Reich discussed early progress in Data Seeds, describing the market as having an "incredible growth potential" and stating, "The appetite for AI training data is virtually insatiable, and we are productizing offerings, we believe can meet the needs of model builders and doing so intelligently and cost effectively." He said the company is "building an off-the-shelf or OTS catalog to drive down cost and accelerate order delivery," while noting revenue in the segment remains lumpy.
  • Reich identified that "our innovation team is humming," with two new alpha products launched this quarter, aiming for up to six for the fiscal year. He said, "Syncat, our first release under the product innovation team framework did not deliver the KPIs we were shooting for and we are ceasing development of this product."
  • Reich indicated that GuruShots "appears to be stabilizing and is being operated conservatively following last year's restructuring," and that cash strengthened to $19.1 million with no debt. He added, "we are now paying a quarterly dividend while continuing to invest in innovation and repurchasing shares when the market conditions are right."
  • CFO Yi Tsai reported, "Total revenue for the second quarter was $8.3 million, up 18.3% from last year." He highlighted that "Zedge Marketplace revenue was up over 21% year-over-year driven by strong advertising CPMs and subscription revenue." Tsai also noted, "Zedge+ subscription revenue increased 33% year-over-year, and our net active subscriber base grew 49%, reaching nearly 1.2 million subscribers."

Outlook

  • Management expects Data Seeds to "increase its contribution in the second half of fiscal 2026." Tsai also cautioned that last year included a one-time benefit to revenue of $450,000 related to an integration bonus from an ad partner that will not repeat in Q3 2026. Reich stated the company will continue to "strengthen monetization in the marketplace, build Data Seeds deliberately and expand our innovation pipeline in a disciplined way."

Financial Results

  • Tsai reported "deferred revenue, which primarily represents subscription-related revenue reached $6 million, up 5% sequentially and 39% year-over-year." He noted, "Zedge Premium GTV was up 15.7% from the year ago quarter and ARPMAU increased 47.6%."
  • Cost of revenue was "6.8% of revenue, which was up from 6.4% last year due to the reduction in partner discounts from Google Cloud Services as well as the introduction of Tapedeck licensing fees and Data Seeds production costs."
  • SG&A decreased about 6% to $6.7 million, reflecting restructuring savings offset by investment in Data Seeds and Tapedeck. GAAP loss from operations was $2.9 million compared to a loss of $2.2 million last year. The quarter included a $3.7 million asset impairment charge related to Emojipedia. GAAP net loss and loss per share were $2.3 million and $0.18, respectively. Non-GAAP net income was $0.8 million and EPS was $0.06. Adjusted EBITDA was $1.1 million, compared to negative $0.1 million last year.
  • Cash flow from operations was $0.9 million and free cash flow was $0.8 million for the quarter. The company ended the quarter with $19.1 million in cash and cash equivalents and no debt.

Q&A

  • Allen Klee, Maxim Group: Asked about the Data Seeds business model, margin expectations, and pipeline size. Reich replied, "the margins have been attractive... margins are also dependent on what type of deal it is, off-the-shelf will command a lower margin than custom made by and large, and depending on whether or not there is a middle man, meaning a marketplace that's evolved, that also impacts margin."
  • Klee: Inquired about expansion into data types beyond photos. Reich explained that Zedge is "focusing on multimodal data that can be images, audio and/or video," noting recent completion of a proof of concept on video and ongoing talks regarding audio.
  • Klee: Requested details on new alpha product launches. Reich said, "the 2 new ones literally are fresh out of the starting gate" and emphasized a modular, data-driven approach to product selection and rapid development.
  • Klee: Questioned subscription revenue momentum. Reich attributed ongoing growth to "our ongoing investment in optimizing our subscription offering and trying to find those pockets of prospective subscribers that will be attracted to what we had to offer."
  • Klee: Asked about Zedge Marketplace drivers. Reich pointed to the overlap of Q2 with year-end advertising spend and "a lot of work on the data science side in order to better segment users and optimize their performance from a monetization perspective."
  • Klee: Probed user acquisition strategy. Reich said, "marketing, find new marketing mechanisms... influencer market," new product features to drive daily engagement, and data science projects to "better isolate prospective new users."
  • Klee: Inquired about Tapedeck progress. Reich mentioned positive KPIs and a strategic shift toward expanding the music catalog rather than ongoing product development.
  • Klee: Asked about the importance of discovery for Tapedeck. Reich described in-app artist discovery features and direct artist partnerships to drive user engagement.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on execution details and strategic priorities, with questions often seeking clarification on growth drivers, margins, and product innovation rather than expressing overt skepticism or concern.
  • Management's tone was confident during prepared remarks and remained measured but upbeat throughout the Q&A. Reich repeatedly emphasized discipline, operational focus, and a data-driven approach. Phrases such as "we believe that balance positions us well for the remainder of fiscal 2026" and "we are still in the midst of refining that process" demonstrate both confidence and ongoing evaluation.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone shifted from more frequent questions about operational risks and visibility to a greater focus on scaling opportunities and execution, while management's confidence remained steady, with more explicit references to infrastructure building and selective investment.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from emphasizing a "robust pipeline" and early Data Seeds traction to a more deliberate approach to scaling Data Seeds, with explicit mention of building a high-quality outbound pipeline and operational infrastructure.
  • Strategic focus sharpened on disciplined innovation, with the discontinuation of Syncat illustrating a willingness to cut underperforming products quickly. There is increased attention to monetization quality and higher-value user acquisition.
  • Analysts shifted focus from near-term risks (e.g., restructuring, segment losses) to detailed questions about product pipeline, margin structure, and growth levers in Data Seeds and Tapedeck.
  • Key metrics such as revenue growth, subscription growth, and ARPMAU saw notable year-over-year and sequential gains, while cost discipline continued through restructuring savings and SG&A reductions.
  • Management maintained a steady, confident tone, reinforcing disciplined capital allocation and operational rigor as the foundation of ongoing growth initiatives.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted "structural headwinds tied to the evolving field of search" for Emojipedia, resulting in a $3.7 million noncash impairment charge.
  • Revenue from Data Seeds remains "lumpy at this stage," with the challenge being "making the right bets, continuing to grow our library of relevant content and executing well in a rapidly developing market."
  • Tsai cautioned that last year's one-time $450,000 integration bonus from an ad partner will not recur in Q3 2026.
  • The company is being selective in Data Seeds opportunities, focusing only on feasible deals to avoid overextension and protect profitability.

Final Takeaway

Zedge management highlighted record monetization results and continued progress in subscription and marketplace revenue, while maintaining strong discipline in innovation and operational investment. The company is building infrastructure to scale its AI data business, making selective bets and emphasizing quality over volume in both user acquisition and product development. Strategic shifts include the discontinuation of underperforming initiatives and a heightened focus on cost control, while capital returns and innovation investments remain priorities. Management's tone signals a balanced approach to growth and profitability for the remainder of fiscal 2026.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Champions Oncology outlines full year revenue growth and positive adjusted EBITDA targets amid early traction in data business
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Champions Oncology (CSBR) Q3 2026

Management View

  • CEO Robert Brainin opened the call by highlighting three main takeaways: record services revenue for the quarter, the third consecutive quarter of positive adjusted EBITDA, and early momentum in the data business with new deals closed. He stated, "while quarterly revenue can fluctuate in our business, we remain on track for full year revenue growth and full year positive adjusted EBITDA while continuing to invest in both our data platform and our discovery therapeutics subsidiary." He emphasized the resilience of customer relationships and noted, "Our PDX Bank remains a true differentiator in the market. And as customer budgets stabilize, we continue to see bookings convert into revenue." Brainin also reported, "we are beginning to see early momentum in our data business, including new deals closed during the quarter and additional revenue expected in the fourth quarter."
  • Brainin described progress on the company's target discovery subsidiary, Corellia, mentioning that feedback is positive from potential partners and that funding for Corellia is part of fiscal 2027 plans, but timing for external financing remains uncertain.
  • CFO David Miller reported, "Total revenue for the quarter was $16.6 million compared to $17 million in the prior year period, a decrease of approximately 3%. However, the mix of revenue this quarter is important to understand. Our core study revenue reached a record $16.6 million compared to $12.6 million in the year ago period, representing growth of approximately 32%." Miller added, "We did not recognize any data revenue from our nascent data platform this quarter... which accounts for the overall year-over-year revenue decline."

Outlook

  • Brainin reiterated, "we remain on track for full year revenue growth and full year positive adjusted EBITDA, all while continuing to invest in both our data business and Corellia without dilution of Champions' shares."
  • He also said, "While EBITDA remains somewhat suppressed in the near term as we continue investing in these growth drivers, we expect the payoff from those investments to begin showing up in fiscal 2027 with more meaningful acceleration in fiscal 2028."
  • The company anticipates recognizing revenue from a newly closed 6-figure data deal in the fourth quarter and incremental revenue from a previously announced large data deal.

Financial Results

  • Miller reported, "Our core study revenue reached a record $16.6 million compared to $12.6 million in the year ago period, representing growth of approximately 32%."
  • He noted, "Cost of sales for the quarter was $8.8 million compared to $6.6 million in the prior year period, resulting in a gross margin of 47% compared to 61% last year. It's important to highlight that more than $2 million of cost of sales in the quarter was attributable to outsourced laboratory work primarily related to radiolabeling workflows."
  • Miller explained, "Operating expenses for the quarter were $7.2 million compared to $5.3 million in the prior year period. The increase reflects investments aligned with our strategic priorities."
  • Net cash used in operating activities for the quarter was $1.4 million, with an ending cash balance of $7.1 million and no debt.

Q&A

  • There were no analyst questions during the session. The operator confirmed, "And there were no questions currently from the lines. I will now hand the call back to Rob Brainin for closing remarks."

Sentiment Analysis

  • There were no analyst questions or comments, so no analyst sentiment is detectable in this quarter’s call.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone in both prepared remarks and closing comments. Brainin stated, "Overall, we're pleased with the progress we're making as we scale the core services business while booting the longer-term growth opportunities in data and drug discovery."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence remained steady and focused on execution, but with a notable emphasis on early data business momentum and increasing investment in strategic areas.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter highlighted record core services revenue and positive adjusted EBITDA for a third consecutive quarter, while noting a slight overall revenue decline year-over-year due to a large data deal in the prior year period.
  • Core study revenue grew substantially from the prior year, but gross margin decreased due to higher outsourced costs, specifically in radiolabeling.
  • Strategic focus shifted to scaling the core services business, building momentum in the data platform, and progressing Corellia funding discussions.
  • There was no analyst participation this quarter, compared to active Q&A in the previous quarter.
  • Management’s tone remains confident and focused on long-term growth drivers.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged that quarterly revenue can be "somewhat lumpy" and that timing of study progression and completion can cause fluctuations.
  • Gross margins were negatively impacted by outsourced laboratory costs associated with radiolabeling workflows, with management stating these costs are expected to decline as work is brought in-house.
  • The timing of external funding for Corellia remains uncertain, with Brainin noting, "I do not have a specific estimate as to when an external financing may occur."

Final Takeaway

Champions Oncology reported another quarter of record core services revenue and positive adjusted EBITDA, with management emphasizing ongoing investments in data and drug discovery as key future growth drivers. The company remains on track for full year revenue growth and positive adjusted EBITDA, expecting tangible benefits from data business deals in the coming quarters and further progress on strategic initiatives such as Corellia. Management highlighted operational leverage and a deliberate approach to capital allocation as central to its strategy moving forward.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Gold continues lackluster showing since start of Iran war while oil keeps climbing
data.description:
Gold and silver futures fell Thursday alongside strong gains in crude oil, a match that has been common since the start of the Iran war.

Despite gold's reputation as a safe-haven trade, the metal has fallen since the U.S. and Israel launched strikes on Iran 12 days ago, while Brent crude oil has climbed 38% during the period to slightly over $100/bbl; gold initially gained, but a selloff trimmed prices to $5,085/oz on March 3, and it has traded between $5,050 and $5,200 this week, even as the conflict has escalated.

Several factors can explain gold's relative weakness, including a stronger dollar and higher Treasury yields, Metals Daily CEO Ross Norman said, adding that rising oil prices could lead to prolonged inflation and potentially higher interest rates, which tend to increase the relative appeal of yielding assets such as government bonds compared to non-yielding precious metals.

"Gold and silver's price movements look lackluster just now, but perhaps that's the way to feel after some epic moves over the last few months," Norman told CNBC.

Gold and silver scored their best-ever monthly dollar gains in February; gold had gained for seven straight months, climbing nearly 60%, and silver increased for 10 consecutive months, skyrocketing 185% during that time.

Bank forecasts remain largely bullish despite the short-term volatility, however, as J.P. Morgan sees gold reaching $6,300/oz by the end of 2026 and Deutsche Bank targets gold at $6,000 by year-end.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for March delivery finished -1% to $5,115.80/oz, and front-month Comex March silver (XAGUSD:CUR) settled -0.4% to $84.67/oz.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Traeger announces 1-for-50 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Traeger (COOK) on Thursday said it will proceed with a 1-for-50 reverse stock split of its outstanding common stock.
  • The reverse stock split is intended to bring the company into compliance with the minimum share price requirement.
  • The reverse stock split is expected to become effective on March 17.
  • COOK -13.76% after hours to $0.5088.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Retail jolt: Amazon plans to move Prime Day into June for the first time
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) plans to shift its annual Prime Day sale to late June from July, sources told Bloomberg. 

A timing change would have broad implications across the retailer sector as thousands of third-party vendors and competitors such as Walmart (WMT), Target (TGT), Costco (COST), Best Buy (BBY), Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS), Etsy (ETSY), Wayfair (W) and Macy's (M) consider if they need to change their summer schedules.

Amazon Prime Day began on July 15, 2015, as a one-day global sales event created to celebrate the 20th anniversary of the company. Originally conceived as a members-only promotion for Prime subscribers, it promised "more deals than Black Friday" and initially ran for 24 hours across nine countries, including the United States, United Kingdom, Germany, and Japan.

In the following years, Prime Day evolved from a single-day experiment into a multi-day, mid-summer retail fixture that Amazon (AMZN) used to drive both sales and Prime subscriptions. By the early 2020s, Prime Day had become one of the largest annual events in e-commerce, often surpassing Amazon's (AMZN) own Black Friday and Cyber Monday performance and reshaping the July promotions and sales events of competitors.

Shares of Amazon (AMZN) edged 0.3% higher in postmarket trading after shedding 1.1% during the regular session.
